commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=67f1962f50fd52a58dfd43e1c110fa235113109c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Anthropic thinking recovery so a control-plane `start` event no longer prevents retry before real content is emitted.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +52. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: the PR's own expected stream is `start`, retry `start`, `done`, and `agentLoop` treats each `start` as a new assistant message. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] There is a narrow mechanical blocker: preserve retry-after-start behavior without emitting duplicate `start` events into agent-loop consumers.

target.title: fix(agents): allow thinking-block recovery retry after control-plane start event (#92201)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the Anthropic thinking-block recovery wrapper (`wrapAnthropicStreamWithRecovery`) so it retries when a pre-stream API error arrives after the control-plane "start" event but before any actual content.
- Previously, `pumpStreamWithRecovery` set `yieldedOutput=true` for every non-error event including `{ type: "start" }`, which unconditionally skipped the recovery retry.
- Now, only real content events (text_delta, thinking_delta, etc.) set `yieldedOutput`, allowing the thinking-block recovery to fire after a "start" event when Anthropic rejects replayed thinking signatures.

## Linked context

Closes #92201

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The `wrapAnthropicStreamWithRecovery` recovery wrapper did not retry when Anthropic rejected replayed thinking blocks with an invalid-signature error, because `yieldedOutput` was already set to `true` by the control-plane `{ type: "start" }` event.
- **Real environment tested:** Unit tests in the embedded-agent-runner test suite (Node 24, Linux).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts
  pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Thinking.test.ts: 43 tests passed (1 new test for `start` event + thinking error = retry; existing test updated to use real content event for the no-retry-after-yielded-output case)
  - Embedded-agent-runner: 1343 tests passed across 81 test files, no regressions
- **Observed result after fix:** The new test "retries when a thinking error arrives after a control-plane start event" confirms that `callCount` is 2 (retry happened) and the retry stream's result is returned instead of the error.
- **What was not tested:** Live end-to-end replay with a corrupted thinking signature against the Anthropic API. This fix is test-covered at the unit level and follows the existing recovery pattern in the codebase.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Full end-to-end proof requires an Anthropic API key and a session with stored corrupted thinking signatures. The unit tests verify the exact code path (start event → error → retry) with the same stream types used in production.

## Tests and validation

```
pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts
  → 43 passed (1 file)
pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/
  → 1343 passed (81 files)
```

No regression in existing tests. New test covers the retry-after-start-event scenario. Updated existing test to use `text_delta` event instead of `start` to exercise the no-retry-after-content-yielded path.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`) — Sessions with Anthropic thinking + corrupted signatures will now auto-recover instead of being permanently stuck.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area? The yieldedOutput logic change; a false positive (skipping retry when we shouldn't) could cause recovery loops or duplicate streaming output.

How is that risk mitigated? The "start" event is always the first event and carries no user-facing content; skipping yieldedOutput for it only allows the retry to fire earlier. The existing `yieldedOutput` guard still works correctly for actual content events (text, thinking, tool calls). The `recoveredAnthropicThinking` flag prevents infinite retry loops.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. Addressed the root cause identified in #92201: the recovery wrapper was applied but skipped because `yieldedOutput` was prematurely set by the control-plane start event.
